title: Ag and Food Statistics: Charting the Essentials - Food Prices and Spending
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: In 2024, U.S. consumers spent $2.58 trillion on food and beverages. U.S. food-at-home prices increased 2.3% in 2025 compared to 2024. Annual food expenditures and inflation rates are tracked from 1964 to 2025.

content.introduction: In 2024, U.S. consumers spent $2.58 trillion on food and beverages. U.S. food-at-home prices increased 2.3% in 2025 compared to 2024. Annual food expenditures and inflation rates are tracked from 1964 to 2025.
body: A collection of over 75 charts and maps presenting key statistics on the farm sector, food spending and prices, food security, rural communities.
A collection of over 75 charts and maps presenting key statistics on the farm sector, food spending and prices, food security,

An official website of the United States government. A **.gov** website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. # Food Prices and Spending. In 2024, U.S. consumers, businesses, and government entities spent $2.58 trillion on food and beverages. ## U.S. food-at-home prices increased 2.3 percent in 2025, compared with 2024. Horizontal comparative bar chart showing annual rates of inflation for major U.S. food-at-home categories from 2024 to 2025. Line chart showing annual percent changes in food, household energy, and motor fuel prices from 2006 to 2025 in the United States. A line graph showing annual food-at-home, food-away-from-home, and total food expenditures in the United States for 1964 to 2024. A line graph showing the percent of disposable personal income U.S. consumers spent on total food, food at home, and food away from home for 1964 to 2024. The vertical bar chart shows the average annual food spending and share of before-tax income spent on food across U.S. households, by income quintile, in 2024.
